Private Travel Proposal · Prepared for the Family

A Rhine River Cruise for John & Carol

Fully-assisted, door-to-door · 12–19 August 2026 · Basel to Amsterdam

Confirmed & booked  |  Balcony Suite 204 · direct flights: Bristol (easyJet) out, Cardiff (KLM) home.

This is a plan built around one promise: John and Carol are looked after from their own front door in Newport to their own front door again — with the drivers and the ship’s porters handling the cases, and a wheelchair for John at every airport. Below is the recommended cruise, the exact journey, what it includes, and what it costs.

The travellers, and what they need.
  • John (79) — an independent walker who paces himself; on long excursions he happily sits and waits. He needs a wheelchair only at airports, where luggage and walking distances combine. He cannot manage a suitcase himself.
  • Carol (77) — genuinely mobile (walks several miles a day), but cannot carry the cases and assist John at the same time.
  • The core requirement — every bag handled for them at every step, and a wheelchair on hand at each airport. A ship with a lift; gentle, optional excursions. Everything possible made effortless.

The Recommendation

Our pick   Scenic “A taste of the Rhine – A wine cruise” — an 8-day, 7-night all-inclusive luxury cruise from Basel to Amsterdam aboard the 163-guest, all-suite Scenic Pearl, departing Wednesday 12 August 2026.

It is the same ship, route and ports as Scenic’s classic “Rhine Highlights” — sailed downstream (Basel→Amsterdam) with a gentle wine theme layered on: vineyard visits and tastings alongside the usual sightseeing. Scenic is simply the best fit for John and Carol — an all-suite ship with a lift and a limited-mobility suite by reception, everything genuinely all-inclusive onboard, and graded excursions so they set their own pace. We arrange the direct flights and the assisted transfers around it.

Why the 12 August sailing. It’s the best-value week of the summer — Scenic’s current sale brings a Balcony Suite for the two of them to £5,790 — and an August trip keeps September free for Carol’s brother Brian’s 80th in Jesolo. The one thing we plan around: the direct Bristol–Basel flight runs Mondays only for this week, so they fly out Monday and enjoy two gentle, tram-based days in Basel before boarding — a restful start, not a rushed one.

The Ship & Your Suite

Scenic Pearl is a 163-guest, all-suite “Space-Ship” — small, calm and beautifully finished, with a lift between all interior decks and a limited-mobility suite by reception. Every stateroom is a suite with a butler; the Balcony Suite has Scenic’s signature full-width “Sun Lounge” window that lowers at the touch of a button.

The cabin to reserve: a Balcony Suite on the middle (Sapphire) Deck — category B, the full 205 sq ft suite with the Sun Lounge. The main restaurant is on this same deck, so it’s the least-walking deck on the ship. Two equally good spots (same suite, same price): of the remaining Category B suites, cabin 204 (or 203) — forward, just steps from the restaurant.

Ship & suite photographs © Scenic (official). Shown for illustration; the exact suite is confirmed on booking.

The Journey — Door to Door

Why Monday, and why two nights in Basel. easyJet is the only nonstop Bristol–Basel carrier, and it flies just twice a week — Mondays and Fridays. The Monday 10 Aug flight is the one that lands in time to board on the Wednesday; the next nonstop isn’t until the Friday. So the two Basel nights are set by the timetable, not by choice — and can’t be trimmed without switching to a connecting flight with a change of planes. If flying out closer to the cruise ever comes up, that’s the answer — this Monday flight is the only nonstop that fits.

Every transfer is assisted. The drivers load and unload the cases and the ship’s porters take them to the cabin; John has a wheelchair at each airport through “special assistance” that Scenic arranges. The chauffeur stays with them until airport staff take over, so there’s no moment stuck alone with the bags.

  1. Collected at the Newport front door by a private car we arrange, and driven to Bristol Airport (~45 min), dropping level and step-free at the “Drop & Go” car park by the terminal. The driver handles the bags and walks them to the assistance point.
  2. easyJet flight, Bristol → Basel (direct, ~1h40). A pre-booked wheelchair (free, by law) takes them and their bags through a dedicated assistance security lane to the gate and onto the plane — no steps, no queues, no outdoor trek.
  3. Met off the plane at Basel and taken to arrivals with the bags; a private car is waiting for the short ~15-min ride into the city (Basel’s own airport is just 4 km from town).
  4. Two nights at the air-conditioned Radisson Blu (central, a step from the tram) — a gentle, tram-based couple of days before boarding (see Two Days in Basel below). A private car then takes them to the ship. (Hotel being finalised — held on a free-cancellation rate.)
  5. Embark Scenic Pearl in Basel. Porters take the cases straight to their suite; John walks the gangway with crew on hand.
  6. Seven nights down the Rhine — Strasbourg & Alsace, Baden-Baden, Heidelberg & the vineyards, Koblenz and the castle-lined Rhine Gorge, Cologne — then Amsterdam (full day-by-day below).
  7. Disembark in Amsterdam; a short private car takes them from the ship to Schiphol airport, then a full-service KLM flight to Cardiff (~1h20), airport assistance again — and a private car home to their Newport door (~25 min).

The Whole Trip, Day by Day

The entire journey at a glance, with times where we have them (the cruise days each have a fuller day-by-day further down).

DateWhereThe day
Mon 10 AugNewport → BaselPrivate car to Bristol (~45 min); easyJet 18:20 → 21:00; private car to the Radisson Blu; supper nearby.
Tue 11 AugBaselA free day — the Beyeler museum (tram 6), a 75-min sightseeing day cruise, and dinner at Restaurant Schlüsselzunft.
Wed 12 AugBasel → aboardMorning by the Rhine; ~11:30am private car to the ship; board Scenic Pearl (from noon); welcome reception.
Thu 13 AugStrasbourg & AlsaceCathedral & Petite France, or an Alsace wine-village tasting.
Fri 14 AugBaden-BadenElegant spa town or the thermal baths; evening palace concert with sparkling wine.
Sat 15 AugHeidelberg & the PfalzCastle & old town, or a Riesling vineyard tour & tasting.
Sun 16 AugKoblenz & the Rhine GorgeKoblenz old town; then the castle-lined gorge from the sun deck, wine in hand.
Mon 17 AugCologneOld town & cathedral, a short flat walk from the ship.
Tue 18 AugAmsterdamArrive; a canal cruise or the Fabrique des Lumières light show.
Wed 19 AugAmsterdam → homeStay aboard for breakfast & lunch (cabin vacated in the morning); Scenic’s transfer to the airport; KLM 16:25 → 16:45 to Cardiff; private car home.

The Route

Seven nights down the Rhine, Basel to Amsterdam — through the Rhineland and the Netherlands, with the castle-lined UNESCO gorge at its heart. Numbers mark the day of the cruise.

The Places You’ll See

Destination photos via Wikimedia Commons: Amsterdam © Basile Morin; Cologne © Ahgee; Marksburg/Rhine — Gary Todd (CC0); Heidelberg © Mikheil Baramidze; Baden-Baden © Franzfoto; Strasbourg © Dietmar Rabich; Basel © Matti Blume. Licensed CC BY-SA / CC0.

On the Rhine — Day by Day

Seven nights, Basel to Amsterdam. Each port offers a choice of included excursions (Scenic’s “Freechoice”), graded by effort, plus a signature “Enrich” evening — a private classical concert in a baroque palace — and, on this sailing, wine tastings and a vineyard visit woven through. The last column is written for John: which days he can stroll off the ship, which involve a coach, and which are worth taking gently.

Easy dockside / low-effort   Coach a bus ride to the sight   Take gently cobbles, hills or long walking

DayPort & sailingIncluded excursions (effort)Walk or coach?For John
1
Wed		 Basel (Switzerland) — embark afternoon; overnight in port Board & settle in; welcome reception aboard (Relaxed) Dockside Easy Settle in aboard, rested after the Basel stay.
2
Thu		 Kehl (for Strasbourg, France) & Alsace — sail on in the evening Strasbourg walking tour — Cathedral, Petite France (Active); Alsace wine village + cellar tasting (Moderate, wine); e-bike city tour (Active) Coach short ~15 min across the Rhine into Strasbourg Take gently Strasbourg is flat but cobbled and a fair walk; the Alsace wine-village + cellar tasting is the gentler, more seated choice.
3
Fri		 Rastatt / Plittersdorf (for Baden-Baden) — day ashore Baden-Baden spa-town walk (Relaxed); Caracalla thermal baths (Relaxed); Schloss Favorite palace (Moderate). Evening Scenic Enrich: private concert, Rastatt Baroque Palace, with a sparkling-wine reception (Relaxed, seated) Coach Baden-Baden is ~20 km inland Easy A gentle coach day — Baden-Baden is flat and elegant; the thermal-spa option is very low-effort, and the evening palace concert is all seated.
4
Sat		 Mannheim (for Heidelberg & the Pfalz vineyards) — day ashore Heidelberg orientation walk (Moderate); Heidelberg + Castle (Moderate); Fritz Ritter vineyard tour & Riesling tasting in the Pfalz (Moderate, wine) Coach Heidelberg ~20 km / 30–40 min; the vineyard a similar coach ride Take gently Heidelberg has cobbles and an uphill castle — take the orientation tour, or the town at his own pace. The vineyard tour & tasting is the gentler, mostly-seated alternative.
5
Sun		 Koblenz + Rhine Gorge scenic cruising — sail the UNESCO gorge of castles Koblenz old-town walk (Moderate); Koblenz Wine Museum (Relaxed); Rhine Gorge cruise with a regional-wine tasting & talk on deck (Relaxed, seated) Koblenz walkable (flat riverside); the gorge is enjoyed aboard Easy The loveliest afternoon of the week — seated on the sun deck as the castles pass, glass of Riesling in hand.
6
Mon		 Cologne (Köln) — arrive; evening free; sail overnight to Amsterdam Old Town + Cathedral walking tour (Moderate); Farina fragrance museum (Relaxed) Walkable — ship moors by the old town, ~5–10 min flat to the cathedral Easy A gentle dockside day; old-town squares are cobbled (skip the tower climb).
7
Tue		 Amsterdam — arrive; overnight in port Canal cruise (Relaxed); Fabrique des Lumières digital-art show (Relaxed); old-city walking tour (Moderate) Dockside / very short transfer Easy The canal cruise or light show are seated and a lovely way to finish.
8
Wed		 Amsterdam — disembark after breakfast; direct flight home from Amsterdam — (disembarkation) Transfer to Amsterdam (Schiphol) airport Easy Relaxed departure — the direct flight home runs most days.

The gist for John: the Rhine-Gorge afternoon (Day 5), Cologne (Day 6), Amsterdam (Day 7) and the Baden-Baden spa day (Day 3) are his easy ones. Strasbourg (Day 2) and Heidelberg (Day 4) involve the most walking — and both have a gentler, mostly-seated wine-themed alternative he can take instead. Nothing is compulsory.

Note: This is Scenic’s wine-themed “A taste of the Rhine” sailing (Basel→Amsterdam) — the same ports as the standard Rhine Highlights, with wine tastings and a vineyard visit added. Exact arrival, departure and sailing times are confirmed in the final Scenic documents; excursion menus can vary slightly by departure.

Two Days in Basel

Two relaxed days at the air-conditioned Radisson Blu (central, a step from the Heuwaage tram stop) before boarding. A free BaselCard at check-in makes every tram free, and a private car covers the airport and the ship. Everything’s on tram 6 — the museum, the day cruise and dinner, one tram, no changes. (German-speaking but very English-friendly; the whole cruise runs in English.)

📱 Tap for live directions (public transport):
Hotel → Museum  ·  Museum → Hotel  ·  Hotel → Restaurant  ·  Restaurant → Hotel

The full day (Tuesday) is paced with a rest built in, not a checklist to race through:

TimeTuesday 11 Aug
Mon evePrivate car BSL airport → Radisson Blu (~15 min); light supper at the hotel.
9:30Breakfast at the hotel (included).
10:45Fondation Beyeler (art museum) — from the Heuwaage stop, Tram 6 dir “Riehen, Grenze” (~25 min) to “Fondation Beyeler” (at the door, one level, chairs; CHF 20pp Tuesdays).
12:30Lunch in the museum garden restaurant; Tram 6 back. Rest at the hotel.
15:00Day cruise (optional): Tram 6 a few stops to Schifflände; the 75-min sightseeing cruise, step-free (lift at pier & on board), ~CHF 15pp with the BaselCard, bought at the dock on the day. Or just sit by the Rhine.
18:30Dinner at Restaurant Schlüsselzunft — a special dinner in the gourmet Zunftstube of Basel’s 600-year-old guild house; tram one stop to Barfüsserplatz, or an 8-min flat walk. (Booked, ref eZmT3U; the gourmet room is up a flight of stairs.)
Wed ~11:30Leisurely morning; private car to the ship (St. Johann dock); board from noon.

Note: the day cruise is the sightseeing boat from Schifflände — a different dock from where the ship boards (the St. Johann landing stage), which the private car handles.  ☐ To book: the two UK car transfers (the Tuesday dinner at Schlüsselzunft is booked).

Who Sorts What

Three simple buckets: what the cruise fare covers, what we arrange around it, and the few things John & Carol handle themselves.

Included in the cruise

  • ✓ 7-night cruise, Balcony Suite 204
  • ✓ Butler; all meals aboard
  • ✓ All drinks all day (incl. wine tastings)
  • ✓ Every excursion — sightseeing & wine
  • ✓ All tips & gratuities
  • ✓ Palace concert; Wi-Fi; laundry
  • ✓ Porters take the cases to the cabin
  • ✓ Direct flights (easyJet + KLM), seats & bags
  • ✓ Airport wheelchair & special assistance
  • ✓ Transfer to the airport in Amsterdam

What we’ll take care of

  • ✓ Booking the cruise & Balcony Suite
  • ✓ The Radisson Blu (2 nights) & the Basel plan
  • ✓ The private cars, Newport ↔ airports
  • ✓ The private cars in Basel (airport & ship)
  • ✓ The dinner reservations
  • ✓ Their phone/data, set up before they go

What John & Carol do

  • • Check their annual travel insurance covers the trip & carry the details
  • • Passports in date; pack medicines in hand luggage
  • • Be ready at the front door — the car does the rest

On the luggage, honestly: the drivers load and unload the cases, the airline’s assistance staff help with them through the airport, and the ship’s porters take them to the cabin — so they’re not dragging bags around, though at check-in the cases do get handed over to the airline as normal. On the flights: we book the direct easyJet (out) and KLM (home) on Scenic’s no-fly fare, so the routing and days are ours to choose; easyJet is low-cost, so hold bags & seats-together are pre-booked as small add-ons.

The Budget — all-in for the pair

Every transfer is a private Mercedes V-Class van — roomy, easy to step into, with room for both cases and a driver to handle them: Newport→Bristol and Cardiff→Newport (Regal), Basel airport/ship and Amsterdam→Schiphol (Blacklane). The car figures below are firm quotes now, not estimates — only the flights and hotel remain as ranges until booked.
ItemCost
Scenic cruise — Balcony Suite 204, 12 Aug (all-inclusive onboard + Scenic-arranged direct flights)£5,790
Flights, 2 pax — easyJet Bristol→Basel + KLM Amsterdam→Cardiff (direct, both confirmed; Scenic-arranged & included — 23kg hold + cabin bags, speedy boarding, seats)included
Private Mercedes V-Class transfers (Newport→Bristol; Basel airport→hotel & hotel→ship; Cardiff→Newport — Amsterdam→airport by Scenic)£466
Basel day cruise (sightseeing; optional — paid at the dock on the day)£30
Radisson Blu Basel — Junior Suite, 2 nights (incl. breakfast; air-conditioned; + ~CHF 17 city tax paid at the hotel)£462
Meals in Basel — supper, lunch & a special dinner, coffees (breakfasts included at the hotel; Wed lunch aboard the ship)£300
Airport wheelchair & special assistance (arranged by Scenic)Free
Travel insurance (already covered by the existing worldwide annual policy)Covered
Total, the pair — true door-to-door≈ £7,050
On the price. The £5,790 is the confirmed, booked price for cabin 204 — Scenic’s Summer Sale took £2,600pp off the brochure fare, and the direct flights are included. Paid in full at booking.

Honest Notes

  • Flights sorted: easyJet Bristol→Basel direct out and KLM Amsterdam→Cardiff home — both from close-to-home airports, both nonstop. easyJet runs this route Mon/Fri only, so they fly Monday and have two gentle days in Basel. Bristol is ~45 min from Newport; the tired homeward drive from Cardiff is only ~25 min.
  • Bristol’s “shambles” reputation doesn’t apply to assisted travel. The chauffeur drops step-free at the “Drop & Go” car park by the terminal; pre-booked special assistance (a free legal right) meets them there with a wheelchair and takes them — and the bags — through a dedicated security lane to the aircraft. No steps, no outdoor trek.
  • The price is confirmed — £5,790 is the booked price for cabin 204 on 12 Aug, with the direct flights included. The cars are firm quotes; the hotel and Basel meals are firm allowances.
  • River berths in Basel and Amsterdam are assigned shortly before sailing; occasionally a ship moors a short drive out. Our transfers absorb this either way.
  • The open-air sun deck is reached by stairs on essentially every river ship (they sit low to pass under bridges). The interior lift serves all cabin and dining decks — no stairs between their suite, the restaurant and reception.
